title: Houses for rent near the airport (Hopkins)
metaTitle: Houses for Rent Near Cleveland Hopkins Airport
metaDescription: Find houses for rent near Cleveland Hopkins International Airport in Brook Park and Berea, with RTA Red Line transit and Section 8-friendly options.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent near cleveland hopkins airport
answerBox: Cleveland Hopkins International Airport sits in the far southwest corner of Cleveland's Hopkins planning area, directly on GCRTA's Red Line, which runs straight through downtown. Nearby communities include Brook Park and Berea. Rent Finder Cleveland manages Section 8-friendly rental homes across greater Cleveland, including some on the west side closer to this corridor.

body: Cleveland Hopkins International Airport occupies **Hopkins**, one of Cleveland's 34 official Statistical Planning Areas, in the far southwest corner of the city near the I-71 corridor. The neighboring **Bellaire-Puritas** planning area, just to the north, also runs along that same I-71/airport corridor.

Just outside the city line, **Berea** (home to Baldwin Wallace University) and **Brook Park** border the airport property, giving renters who work at or near Hopkins several nearby municipalities to consider beyond Cleveland's city limits.

Renters searching for a house in this corridor are effectively choosing between Cleveland's own Hopkins and Bellaire-Puritas neighborhoods and the surrounding suburbs, since the airport property itself sits at the edge of the city rather than in the middle of dense residential blocks. That makes transit access, rather than a single named district, the more useful way to think about "airport-area" renting.

sections.h2: Getting downtown from the airport area
body: GCRTA's **Red Line** is a heavy-rail route running from the airport ("Airport–Windermere") straight through downtown's Tower City to Windermere station in East Cleveland, with trains roughly every 15 minutes throughout the day. It's the only rapid-transit line in the country that connects directly to its city's main airport terminal, which makes it a genuine car-free commute option for airport-area workers, not just travelers.

rows: Cleveland Hopkins Airport ↔ Windermere (East Cleveland), via downtown Tower City
Only U.S. rapid-transit line with a direct downtown-to-main-airport connection

sections.h2: What do rental houses cost in southwest Cleveland?
body: Cleveland's citywide median asking rent was **$1,250 a month** per Zumper's July 4, 2026 report, with 3-bedroom houses running closer to $1,350 — a useful benchmark for the larger homes airport-area families typically look for. Because houses in this corridor tend to be older and smaller than the newer complexes RentCafe's dataset leans on, expect prices nearer Zumper's figures than RentCafe's July 2, 2026 average of $1,564 for professionally managed apartments.

Zumper's 2-bedroom figure of $1,100 is also a reasonable starting point for airport-corridor houses, since two-bedroom homes make up the largest share of Cleveland's rental-house stock overall. Actual prices in Brook Park, Berea, and Cleveland's own southwest neighborhoods will vary with lot size, age, and whether a garage is included, so treat these citywide numbers as a planning benchmark rather than a quote for any specific address.

sections.h2: Does Rent Finder Cleveland have houses near the airport?
body: Our 90+ rental homes are concentrated on Cleveland's east and southeast side, but the portfolio also includes **[west-side rental homes](/cleveland-rentals/houses/houses-for-rent-west-side-cleveland/)** in Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway (ZIP 44102) and Old Brooklyn/Brooklyn Centre (ZIP 44109) — both closer to the southwest/airport corridor than our east-side core.

We don't currently promise specific availability near Brook Park or Berea, but if you're renting for airport-area work, **[book a showing](/p/book-showing)** and share your commute needs and budget so we can point you to the closest current option.

Every rental home we manage, regardless of neighborhood, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready. The Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA) runs the county's voucher program, but voucher protections vary by city: **Ohio has no statewide source-of-income law**, and Cleveland proper doesn't ban source-of-income discrimination, so ask any landlord in Brook Park or Berea directly whether they accept a voucher. See our **[Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/)** for the full picture.

faq.q: Are there houses for rent near Cleveland Hopkins Airport?
faq.a: Yes — the airport sits in Cleveland's Hopkins planning area, bordered by Bellaire-Puritas within the city and by Berea and Brook Park just outside it. Rent Finder Cleveland's nearest homes are on Cleveland's west side, in the Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway and Old Brooklyn areas; book a showing to check current options closest to the airport corridor.

faq.q: How do I get from the airport area to downtown Cleveland without a car?
faq.a: GCRTA's Red Line runs directly from the airport through downtown's Tower City to Windermere in East Cleveland, with trains about every 15 minutes all day. It's the only U.S. rapid-transit line built with a direct connection to its city's main airport.

faq.q: What communities are near Cleveland Hopkins Airport?
faq.a: Berea, home to Baldwin Wallace University, and Brook Park both border the airport property just outside Cleveland's city limits, while Bellaire-Puritas is the Cleveland neighborhood immediately to the north along the I-71 corridor.

faq.q: What do houses rent for near the airport?
faq.a: Cleveland's citywide median asking rent is about $1,250 a month per Zumper's July 4, 2026 report, with 3-bedroom houses closer to $1,350. Older housing stock in this corridor tends to price nearer that figure than the higher averages reported for newer managed apartments.

faq.q: Do airport-area rentals from Rent Finder Cleveland accept Section 8?
faq.a: Every home we manage accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, regardless of neighborhood. Cleveland itself has no source-of-income protection ordinance, so if you're looking outside our current listings in Brook Park or Berea, confirm voucher acceptance directly with that landlord.
